title: BES Cyber System Restore Drill ‚Äî RTO Measurement

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NERC CIP CIP-009-6 R5 Part 5.1 is enforced ‚Äî Splunk UC-22.13.48: BES Cyber System Restore Drill ‚Äî RTO Measurement.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.13.48' running on index=itsm sourcetype=restore_drill, index=apps sourcetype=http:ping, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted ‚Äî SME review required.

spl:
index=itsm sourcetype=restore_drill bes_system=* earliest=-400d
| eval start=strptime(started_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval end=strptime(restored_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval rto_min=round((end-start)/60,2)
| lookup cip009_rto_targets.csv bes_system OUTPUT target_min
| where rto_min > target_min
| table drill_id, bes_system, rto_min, target_min, restored_at
description: We compute restore-time metrics from drill tickets and application availability pings to evidence recovery capability testing.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NERC CIP compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes apps, itsm and sourcetypes http:ping`, restore_drill, restore_drill`. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Populate RTO targets from recovery plans. (2) Validate timestamps from orchestration tools. (3) Track remediation for misses. (4) Annual leadership briefing export. Indexes required: `index=apps`, `index=itsm`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=http:ping``, `sourcetype=restore_drill`, `sourcetype=restore_drill``; Lookups: `cip009_rto_targets`, `cip009_rto_targets.csv`.
visualization: Column chart (RTO vs target), Table (misses), Single value (worst RTO). Key fields: `bes_system`, `drill_id`, `end`, `restored_at`, `rto_min`, `start`.
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern ‚Äî correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
grandmaExplanation: We watch for we compute restore-time metrics from drill tickets and application availability pings to evidence recovery capability testing. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263).
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=itsm` `sourcetype=restore_drill`, `index=apps` `sourcetype=http:ping`.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
(1) Populate RTO targets from recovery plans. (2) Validate timestamps from orchestration tools. (3) Track remediation for misses. (4) Annual leadership briefing export.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype=restore_drill bes_system=* earliest=-400d
| eval start=strptime(started_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval end=strptime(restored_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval rto_min=round((end-start)/60,2)
| lookup cip009_rto_targets.csv bes_system OUTPUT target_min
| where rto_min > target_min
| table drill_id, bes_system, rto_min, target_min, restored_at
```

Understanding this SPL

**BES Cyber System Restore Drill ‚Äî RTO Measurement (CIP-009-6 R5 Part 5.1)** ‚Äî We compute restore-time metrics from drill tickets and application availability pings to evidence recovery capability testing.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=itsm` `sourcetype=restore_drill`, `index=apps` `sourcetype=http:ping`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: itsm; **sourcetype**: restore_drill. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=itsm, sourcetype=restore_drill, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **start** ‚Äî applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **end** ‚Äî applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **rto_min** ‚Äî applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
‚Ä¢ Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where rto_min > target_min` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: bes_system, drill_id, end, restored_at, rto_min, start, target_min.

Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Column chart (RTO vs target), Table (misses), Single value (worst RTO)
